The ***Journal of Accounting and Economics*** is a [peer-reviewed](/wiki/Peer_review "Peer review") [academic journal](/wiki/Academic_journal "Academic journal") focusing on the fields of [accounting](/wiki/Accounting "Accounting") and [economics](/wiki/Economics "Economics"). The [editors-in-chief](/wiki/Editors-in-chief "Editors-in-chief") are J. Core ([Massachusetts Institute of Technology](/wiki/Massachusetts_Institute_of_Technology "Massachusetts Institute of Technology")), E. deHaan ([Stanford University](/wiki/Stanford_Graduate_School_of_Business "Stanford Graduate School of Business")), and W. R. Guay ([University of Pennsylvania](/wiki/University_of_Pennsylvania_Wharton_School "University of Pennsylvania Wharton School")).

According to the *[Journal Citation Reports](/wiki/Journal_Citation_Reports "Journal Citation Reports")*, the journal has a 2021 [impact factor](/wiki/Impact_factor "Impact factor") of 7.239.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Journal_of_Accounting_and_Economics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Reception")]

In a study by Kalaitzidakis et al. (2003), the Journal of Accounting and Economics ranked 88th out of 159 publications evaluated, but in an updated study by Kalaitzidakis et al. (2011) to 77th place out of 209 publications compared. In the economics publication ranking of the Tinbergen Institute at the University of Amsterdam, the Journal of Accounting and Economics is listed in category A (“very good general economics journals and top journals in the respective field”).[[2]](#cite_note-2)
